summary: PR #2319 replaced four fighter placeholder declarations with C bodies across Kirby, Crazy Hand, common grab escape, and fighter color animation code, plus one Crazy Hand attribute type correction. Bot output confirms three new 100% matches and one Kirby function improvement to 95.54%, while the PR body claims fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions.

key_files.role: Added ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420 body for Kirby copied-hat diffuse color updates using FtPartsVisLookup iteration and material null checks.
Added ftCh_Init_80157170 body for Crazy Hand heavy damage flinch state setup, animation, and SFX.
Changed ftCrazyHandAttributes.x24 from s32 to float, used by the new Crazy Hand body.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCrazyHandAttributes x24 s32 to float

key_files.role: Added fn_800DC070 body for grab escape CaptureJump velocity/state transition.
Added ft_800C0098 body for clearing fighter color animation and conditionally reapplying Cloaking Device spycloak overlay; function wrapped in pragmas.

what_changed: Replaced placeholder `/// #fn_800DC070` with a function that gets the Fighter, reads `victim_gobj`, clears common state with `ftCommon_8007D5D4`, sets escape velocity from `p_ftCommonData->x374/x378`, clears `mv.co.buryjump.x0`, calls `ftCo_800DC920`, and enters `ftCo_MS_CaptureJump`.

Replaced placeholder `/// #ftCh_Init_80157170` with a function that reads Crazy Hand attributes, computes `mv.ch.unk0.xC` values, enters `ftMh_MS_Damage2`, calls `ftAnim_8006EBA4`, and plays SFX `0x4E207`.
Changed `ftCrazyHandAttributes.x24` from `s32` to `float`, aligning it with assignment into a floating movement/vector field in the new Crazy Hand body.

Replaced placeholder `/// #ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420` with nested loops over `FtPartsVisLookup` entries to find Kirby hat DOBJs, safely traverse DObj/MObj/material pointers, and write a u32 color value into `mat->diffuse`.
Replaced placeholder `/// #ft_800C0098` with a color-animation reset/reapply routine, wrapped by `#pragma push`, `#pragma auto_inline off`, `#pragma global_optimizer off`, and `#pragma pop`.
smart_moves: Kept unknown/global function names address-style (`fn_800DC070`, `ft_800C0098`, `ftCh_Init_80157170`) rather than inventing stronger semantics.
Used short local role names where roles are clear (`fp`, `gobj`, `victim`, `attrs`, `lookup`, `entry`, `mobj`, `mat`).
Updated the Crazy Hand attribute field type in the owning type header instead of compensating locally with casts.
Expressed Kirby hat processing as nested natural loops over lookup entries and per-entry indices.
Scoped the optimizer/inline pragmas tightly around only `ft_800C0098` with push/pop rather than applying a broader file-level pragma.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a matched body reveals an attribute field is used as a float, update the owning type definition rather than leaving an integer field and casting at use sites.
Repeated object/material updates over lookup tables can be represented with normal nested loops and explicit null checks.
PR-body verification claims should be compared with bot report evidence; here the body claims 100.0 fuzzy match for all functions, while the bot reports three 100% matches and one 95.54% improvement.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Verification fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions
text_corpus issue_comment: 3 new matches and 1 improvement in unmatched item

naming_conventions.convention: Use `fp` for `Fighter*` and `gobj` for fighter/HSD game objects when the local role is clear.
Use role names for local pointers when semantics are directly supported by the accessed field or table, such as `victim`, `attrs`, `lookup`, `entry`, `mobj`, and `mat`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_MObj* mobj and HSD_Material* mat

naming_conventions.convention: Retain address-style names for functions and fields whose stronger semantics are not proven in the PR slice.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: x5AC, x2226_b4, x408, x488, x508

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scoped `#pragma auto_inline off` and `#pragma global_optimizer off` around `ft_800C0098`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The loaded slice does not include adjacent-function objdiff/regression evidence for the pragma exception; current standards treat pragmas as exceptions requiring narrow proof.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used assignment expression inside `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` argument list for `fp->mv.ch.unk0.xC.z = ftCh_Init_804DA070`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Supported only as observed PR code shape; no separate review discussion explains why this exact expression form was required.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Wrote a packed color value through `*(u32*) &mat->diffuse = *arg1`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The PR slice supports this as the landed code, but does not include a type discussion for `diffuse` or an explanation of the cast.

follow_up_queries: ft_800C0098 pragma global_optimizer off auto_inline off
fn_800DC070 CaptureJump self_vel p_ftCommonData x374 x378
FtPartsVisLookup TempS Kirby hat x14 data

notes: Diff evidence is complete for the five changed files in the slice.
No review comments or human review feedback are available in the loaded evidence.

Verification evidence conflicts: PR body says all four functions have `fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0`, but the bot report lists three new 100.00% matches and Kirby `ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420` as an unmatched-item improvement from 0.00% to 95.54%.
The pragma use in `ft_800C0098` is visible in the diff, but the slice does not include narrow adjacent-function regression evidence or rationale.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header type corrections should accompany bodies that prove field type
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2319 updates `ftCrazyHandAttributes.x24` from `s32` to `float` in `types.h` because the matched Crazy Hand body assigns `attrs->x24` into a floating movement/vector field.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Bot reports can narrow generated PR-body match claims
accepted_candidate_records.text: For PR #2319, the generated PR body claims all four functions reached fuzzy 100.0, while the bot report confirms only three new 100% matches and one 95.54% Kirby improvement. Intake should preserve that distinction.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: 3 new matches, 1 improvement to 95.54%

source_update_candidates.title: Fighter matching paths touched by PR #2319
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2319 matched or improved functions in `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100.c`, `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCrazyHand/ftCh_Init.c`, `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_Init.c`, and `src/melee/ft/ftcolanim.c`, with a supporting type change in `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCrazyHand/types.h`.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful searchable path-scope metadata for future fighter matching work; should be curated as a path fact only if the source corpus tracks historical PR touchpoints.

source_update_candidates.title: Pragma exceptions need adjacent verification evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2319 contains a tightly scoped `#pragma auto_inline off` and `#pragma global_optimizer off` around `ft_800C0098`, but the loaded slice does not include rationale or adjacent regression proof. Existing standards already warn that pragmas are exceptions; curator may use this PR as an example of why intake should record missing pragma evidence.
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible example, not a new accepted standard; the PR evidence is insufficient to prove the pragma was unavoidable.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR body's `fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0 for all functions` as the final verification fact without noting the bot report discrepancy.
Do not infer reviewer acceptance rationale; no review comments are present.
Do not generalize `#pragma global_optimizer off` as normal style from this PR; the loaded evidence only shows the pragma was used in one landed function.
Do not rename address-style functions or fields based solely on the descriptive PR body.
